Example: zero inflated Poisson 02

A zero-inflated Poisson regression model for the pscl::bioChemists article-count outcome.

library(XMCMC)
library(coda)
library(pscl)
## Classes and Methods for R originally developed in the
## Political Science Computational Laboratory
## Department of Political Science
## Stanford University (2002-2015),
## by and under the direction of Simon Jackman.
## hurdle and zeroinfl functions by Achim Zeileis.
set.seed(123)

data("bioChemists", package = "pscl")

# Outcome:
# art = count of articles produced during the last 3 years of Ph.D.
y <- as.integer(bioChemists$art)
sex <- as.integer(bioChemists$fem == "Women")
mar <- as.integer(bioChemists$mar == "Single")
nokid <- as.integer(bioChemists$kid5 == 0)
pres <- as.double(bioChemists$phd)
ment <- as.double(bioChemists$ment)

n <- length(y)

cat("n:", n, "\n")
## n: 915
cat("Mean art:", mean(y), "\n")
## Mean art: 1.692896
cat("Observed zero rate:", mean(y == 0L), "\n")
## Observed zero rate: 0.3005464
hist(
  y,
  breaks = seq(-0.5, max(y) + 0.5, by = 1),
  main = "bioChemists: articles in last 3 years of Ph.D.",
  xlab = "art"
)

# -------------------------
# ZIP regression model
# -------------------------
# theta(1) = logit(psi)
# theta(2) = log(lambda intercept)
# theta(3:7) = log(lambda) regression coefficients
#
# z(i) = 0 means structural zero
# z(i) = 1 means Poisson article-producing state

model_src <- '
param theta(7)
dparam z(n, 0, 1)

block theta(j) {
  double il_theta1 = inv_logit(theta(1));
  
  for (i = 1:n) {
    z(i) ~ dbern(il_theta1);

    if (z(i) == 0) {
      if (y(i) != 0) return -INFINITY;
    } else {
      double eeta = exp(theta(2) + theta(3)*sex(i) + theta(4)*mar(i) + theta(5)*nokid(i) + theta(6)*pres(i) + theta(7)*ment(i));
      y(i) ~ dpois(eeta);
    }
  }
}

block z(i) {
  double il_theta1 = inv_logit(theta(1));
  
  z(i) ~ dbern(il_theta1);

  if (z(i) == 0) {
    if (y(i) != 0) return -INFINITY;
  } else {
    double eeta = exp(theta(2) + theta(3)*sex(i) + theta(4)*mar(i) + theta(5)*nokid(i) + theta(6)*pres(i) + theta(7)*ment(i));
    y(i) ~ dpois(eeta);
  }
}
'

model_src <- '
param theta(7)
dparam z(n, 0, 1)

block theta(j) {
  double il_theta1 = inv_logit(theta(1));
  
  for (i = 1:n) {
    z(i) ~ dbern(il_theta1);

    if (z(i) == 0 && y(i) != 0) return -INFINITY;

if (z(i) != 0) {
    double eeta = exp(theta(2)
        + theta(3)*sex(i)
        + theta(4)*mar(i)
        + theta(5)*nokid(i)
        + theta(6)*pres(i)
        + theta(7)*ment(i));

    y(i) ~ dpois(eeta);
}
  }
}

block z(i) {
  double il_theta1 = inv_logit(theta(1));
  
  z(i) ~ dbern(il_theta1);

if (z(i) == 0 && y(i) != 0) return -INFINITY;

if (z(i) != 0) {
    double eeta = exp(theta(2)
        + theta(3)*sex(i)
        + theta(4)*mar(i)
        + theta(5)*nokid(i)
        + theta(6)*pres(i)
        + theta(7)*ment(i));

    y(i) ~ dpois(eeta);
}
}
'

model_src <- '
param theta(7)
dparam z(n, 0, 1)

block theta(j) {
  double il_theta1 = inv_logit(theta(1));

  for (i = 1:n) {
    z(i) ~ dbern(il_theta1);

    if (z(i) == 0) {
      if (y(i) != 0) return -INFINITY;
    } else {
      y(i) ~ dpois(exp(theta(2) + theta(3)*sex(i) + theta(4)*mar(i) + theta(5)*nokid(i) + theta(6)*pres(i) + theta(7)*ment(i)));
    }
  }
}

block z(i) {
  double il_theta1 = inv_logit(theta(1));

  z(i) ~ dbern(il_theta1);

  if (z(i) == 0) {
    if (y(i) != 0) return -INFINITY;
  } else {
    y(i) ~ dpois(exp(theta(2) + theta(3)*sex(i) + theta(4)*mar(i) + theta(5)*nokid(i) + theta(6)*pres(i) + theta(7)*ment(i)));
  }
}
'

mod_data <- list(
  n = n,
  y = y,
  sex = sex,
  mar = mar,
  nokid = nokid,
  pres = pres,
  ment = ment,
  theta_dim = 7L + n
)

fit <- XMCMC(
  model = model_src,
  data = mod_data,
  samples = 10000L,
  burnin = 1000L,
  out = "chain_biochemists_zip_regression.bin"
)

draws <- read_XMCMC("chain_biochemists_zip_regression.bin")

# -------------------------
# Posterior summaries
# -------------------------

psi_draws <- plogis(draws$`theta[1]`)

x_lambda <- cbind(
  intercept = 1,
  sex = sex,
  mar = mar,
  nokid = nokid,
  pres = pres,
  ment = ment
)

beta_cols <- paste0("theta[", 2:7, "]")
beta_draws <- as.matrix(draws[beta_cols])

eta_lambda <- beta_draws %*% t(x_lambda)
lambda_i <- exp(eta_lambda)

expected_art_draws <- rowMeans(lambda_i * psi_draws)
zero_prob_draws <- rowMeans((1 - psi_draws) + psi_draws * exp(-lambda_i))
baseline_lambda_draws <- exp(draws$`theta[2]`)

params <- data.frame(
  psi = psi_draws,
  baseline_lambda = baseline_lambda_draws,
  expected_art = expected_art_draws,
  zero_prob = zero_prob_draws
)

cat("\nPosterior means:\n")
## 
## Posterior means:
print(colMeans(params))
##             psi baseline_lambda    expected_art       zero_prob 
##       0.8443996       1.5829390       1.6713877       0.2882330
cat("\n95% credible intervals:\n")
## 
## 95% credible intervals:
print(apply(params, 2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)))
##             psi baseline_lambda expected_art zero_prob
## 2.5%  0.8032584        1.316710     1.578114 0.2588548
## 50%   0.8442132        1.563553     1.671107 0.2881960
## 97.5% 0.8846159        1.941027     1.767897 0.3183599
cat("\nRegression coefficient posterior summaries:\n")
## 
## Regression coefficient posterior summaries:
coef_draws <- data.frame(
  lambda_intercept = draws$`theta[2]`,
  sex_women = draws$`theta[3]`,
  single = draws$`theta[4]`,
  no_kids_under_5 = draws$`theta[5]`,
  phd_prestige = draws$`theta[6]`,
  mentor_articles = draws$`theta[7]`
)

print(data.frame(
  parameter = names(coef_draws),
  mean = as.numeric(colMeans(coef_draws)),
  q025 = as.numeric(apply(coef_draws, 2, quantile, probs = 0.025)),
  q500 = as.numeric(apply(coef_draws, 2, quantile, probs = 0.500)),
  q975 = as.numeric(apply(coef_draws, 2, quantile, probs = 0.975))
))
##          parameter          mean        q025         q500        q975
## 1 lambda_intercept  0.4541780392  0.27513598  0.446960571  0.66321716
## 2        sex_women -0.2252601042 -0.33742474 -0.226645100 -0.10402753
## 3           single -0.1229249556 -0.26359154 -0.120760672  0.01394652
## 4  no_kids_under_5  0.2246881993  0.09433179  0.222888378  0.35736373
## 5     phd_prestige -0.0003556641 -0.05966231  0.002166455  0.04519529
## 6  mentor_articles  0.0217763716  0.01762551  0.021912748  0.02635048
cat("\nESS:\n")
## 
## ESS:
print(effectiveSize(as.mcmc(cbind(params, coef_draws))))
##              psi  baseline_lambda     expected_art        zero_prob 
##        586.92217         47.04227       2468.14693        851.02937 
## lambda_intercept        sex_women           single  no_kids_under_5 
##         48.75258        745.07781        735.34081        309.03720 
##     phd_prestige  mentor_articles 
##         37.32400         50.63743
cat("\nObserved vs posterior fitted summaries:\n")
## 
## Observed vs posterior fitted summaries:
print(data.frame(
  quantity = c("mean_art", "zero_rate"),
  observed = c(mean(y), mean(y == 0L)),
  posterior_mean = c(mean(params$expected_art), mean(params$zero_prob))
))
##    quantity  observed posterior_mean
## 1  mean_art 1.6928962       1.671388
## 2 zero_rate 0.3005464       0.288233
op <- par(mfrow = c(4, 1), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1))

plot(params$psi, type = "l", main = "psi trace", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")
plot(params$baseline_lambda, type = "l", main = "baseline lambda trace", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "lambda")
plot(params$expected_art, type = "l", main = "Expected articles", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "E[y]")
abline(h = mean(y), col = 2)
plot(params$zero_prob, type = "l", main = "Zero probability", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "Pr(y = 0)")
abline(h = mean(y == 0L), col = 2)

par(op)

op <- par(mfrow = c(4, 1), mar = c(3, 3, 2, 1))

plot(beta_draws[,1], type = "l", main = "beta1", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")
plot(beta_draws[,2], type = "l", main = "beta2", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")
plot(beta_draws[,3], type = "l", main = "beta3", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")
plot(beta_draws[,4], type = "l", main = "beta4", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")

plot(beta_draws[,5], type = "l", main = "beta5", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")
plot(beta_draws[,6], type = "l", main = "beta6", xlab = "Draw", ylab = "psi")

par(op)

# Posterior probability each observation belongs to the Poisson state
z_cols <- paste0("z[", seq_len(n), "]")
z_mean <- colMeans(draws[z_cols])

cat("\nMean posterior Pr(z = 1):\n")
## 
## Mean posterior Pr(z = 1):
print(mean(z_mean))
## [1] 0.8443473
cat("\nMean posterior Pr(z = 1 | art = 0):\n")
## 
## Mean posterior Pr(z = 1 | art = 0):
print(mean(z_mean[y == 0L]))
## [1] 0.4821011
cat("\nMean posterior Pr(z = 1 | art > 0):\n")
## 
## Mean posterior Pr(z = 1 | art > 0):
print(mean(z_mean[y > 0L]))
## [1] 1
unlink("chain_biochemists_zip_regression.bin")
unlink("chain_biochemists_zip_regression.bin.metadata.rds")
unlink("chain_biochemists_zip_regression.bin.param_names.rds")
